quizQuestion:
El libro está aquí. ¿{___} ves?
(The book is here. Do you see it?)
sentenceTranslation: The book is here. Do you see it?
explanation: lo — direct object pronoun for masculine singular nouns. Replaces 'el libro' (the book). Position: before the conjugated verb.

quizQuestion:
¿Conoces a María? Sí, {___} conozco.
(Do you know María? Yes, I know her.)
sentenceTranslation: Do you know María? Yes, I know her.
grammarPointLabel: Direct object pronoun: la (fem. sing. / person)
explanation: la — direct object pronoun for feminine singular. Replaces 'a María'. Note the personal 'a' before a person as direct object.

quizQuestion:
Tengo los boletos. {___} compré ayer.
(I have the tickets. I bought them yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: I have the tickets. I bought them yesterday.
explanation: los — direct object pronoun for masculine plural nouns. Replaces 'los boletos' (the tickets).

quizQuestion:
¿Viste a mis hermanas? Sí, {___} vi ayer.
(Did you see my sisters? Yes, I saw them yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: Did you see my sisters? Yes, I saw them yesterday.
explanation: las — direct object pronoun for feminine plural. Replaces 'a mis hermanas'.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} llamas mañana, por favor?
(Will you call me tomorrow, please?)
sentenceTranslation: Will you call me tomorrow, please?
explanation: me — first person singular direct object pronoun. 'Llamas a mí' → 'me llamas'. Position: before the conjugated verb.

quizQuestion:
{___} llamo ahora mismo.
(I'll call you right now.)
explanation: te — second person singular direct object pronoun. 'Llamo a ti' → 'te llamo'.

quizQuestion:
Quiero {___} antes de comprarlo.
(I want to see it before buying it.)
sentenceTranslation: I want to see it before buying it.
grammarPointLabel: Direct object pronoun attached to infinitive: ver + lo → verlo
explanation: verlo — when a pronoun follows an infinitive, it attaches to the end: ver + lo = verlo. Alternative: also valid before the conjugated verb (lo quiero ver).

quizQuestion:
{___} di el libro a María.
(I gave the book to María.)
sentenceTranslation: I gave the book to María.
explanation: le — indirect object pronoun for él/ella/usted. 'A María' = indirect object → 'le'. Note: IOP comes before DOP. Le + lo/la → se lo/la.

quizQuestion:
{___} dije la verdad a mis padres.
(I told my parents the truth.)
sentenceTranslation: I told my parents the truth.
explanation: les — indirect object pronoun for ellos/ellas/ustedes. 'A mis padres' = indirect object → 'les'.

quizQuestion:
Mis padres {___} dieron un regalo de cumpleaños.
(My parents gave me a birthday present.)
sentenceTranslation: My parents gave me a birthday present.
explanation: me — first person IOP. 'Dieron un regalo a mí' → 'me dieron un regalo'. IOPs always precede the verb.

quizQuestion:
{___} mandé un mensaje esta mañana.
(I sent you a message this morning.)
sentenceTranslation: I sent you a message this morning.
explanation: te — second person IOP. 'Mandé un mensaje a ti' → 'te mandé un mensaje'.

quizQuestion:
Quería decirle la verdad. {___} dije ayer.
(I wanted to tell him the truth. I told it to him yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: I wanted to tell him the truth. I told it to him yesterday.
grammarPointLabel: IOP + DOP: le + lo → se lo
explanation: se lo — when both le/les (IOP) and lo/la/los/las (DOP) appear together, le/les change to 'se'. 'Le lo dije' is incorrect; 'se lo dije' is correct.
